questionHtml: Suggest two reasons, other than elevation, for the differences in nitrogen concentration in leaves of pitcher plants.
markschemeHtml:
1. pitcher plants consume more insects (at different sampling sites);
  2. larger insects contain more nitrogen;
  3. more nitrogen concentration in soil
    OR
    more nitrifying bacteria in the soil
    OR
    water-logged soil has less nitrogen;
  4. use of N/NPK fertilizers
    OR
    leaching of N in different sites;

meta.title: Suggest two reasons, other than elevation, for the differences in nitrogen concentration in leaves of pitcher plants.
breadcrumbLabels: D4.2.8. Eutrophication of aquatic and marine ecosystems due to leaching
